entry.title: SAP Enable Now
entry.content: What is the future of SAP Enable Now? Is there any roadmap with SAP for combining SAP Enable Now and WalkMe in future because there are components of Enable Now that are not available through WalkMe, e.g. the digital learning content such Books and Book Pages and Simulations.

entry.title: Re: 通过项目管理在建工程，就是创建了投资项目后，并且勾选了向下结构传递，一个项目也创建了不同阶段的wbs要素分解，帮助文档中说明，向下机构传递勾选后，其余运行会采用主项目结算规则，然后运行期末结算
entry.content: Hello, thank you very much for your contribution. Could you please let us know whether you wanted to publish this information as a blog or what your question is? Thank you very much. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: [Webinar] Unlocking Potential of Configuration and Security Analysis with SAP Cloud ALM (Korean)
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM Technical and Configuration Deep Dive 2025 

We are thrilled to extend a warm welcome to everyone for our SAP Cloud ALM technical and configuration deep dive webinar series.

Topic

Unlocking the Full Potential of Configuration and Security Analysis with SAP Cloud ALM (Korean)

Agenda

  • SAP Cloud ALM Overview - 5 min
  • Unlocking the Full Potential of Configuration and Security Analysis with SAP Cloud ALM - 50 mins
  • Q&A - 5 mins

Register Now

We look forward to seeing you virtually!

Stay updated on upcoming sessions!

For any further queries please contact us on cloudalm@sap.com

 

If you have any questions about the session topic, please feel free to post them in the comments.

entry.title: Re: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide
entry.content:
Hi @beatebea ,

You can't log into Joule with your S/4 HANA user name and Password. However, you can definitely have seamless Joule experience without having to double log on, if you have SSO setup between your S/4 HANA and Cloud Identity services. 

You must use the same CIS tenant for S/4 and BTP. 

 

Best regards,

Samir

entry.title: Re: BDCQ - Finance - Needs to be in sync with SAP Best Practice Scope Items List
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: Employment Status in Story reports

entry.title: Re: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide
entry.content:
Hi @Rattasak ,

You can remove the UUID condition from the target transformation JSON. Just have "condition": "($.emails EMPTY false). 

Best regards,

Samir

entry.title: SAP DM: Integration Scenario with Stock Room Management?
entry.content:
Hello,

where can i find reliable information about possible integration possibilities with SAP WM / Stock Room Management. EWM is on the roadmap but only in a few years. Is there sth, that can be enabled with ECC / WM including staging with WM TO? and then use this 1:1 in S4/Stockroom Managament? Scenarios like Staging 2.0 are only with EWM possible and slides i could find mention only "IM" and use rough stocks in storage locations.

 

I appreciate expert input and thoughts here.

 

br

Patrick

entry.title: Re: RFOPENPOSTING_FR functionality
entry.content:
Hi @prasadgarude90 

RFOPENPOSTING_FR is basically a legal requirement in France for tax audit reporting.

It can be used for many other balance sheet accounts and not just Customer and Vendor reconciliation accounts. but this is used only for Open item management balance sheet accounts.

Its mostly used for reconciliation accounts because they require open item tracking but it can be used for other balance sheet open item accounts which are require open item tracking. 

I hope this answers your query.

 

 

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2023 Fully-Activated Appliance: Known Issues
entry.content:
Hi, i am having issues installing the 2023 FPS03 Edition. The installation stops at the first step after the extraction of the archives. 

After /hana/shared/HDB/hdblcm/hdblcm --action=register_rename_system

I get an error: 

16:08:51.789 - ERR :   Renaming instance failed
16:08:51.789 - ERR : Renaming master failed!
16:08:51.789 - ERR : Renaming SAP HANA instance failed!
16:08:51.807 - INFO: -----------------------------------------------------------
16:08:51.807 - INFO: Cleaning up configuration changes...
16:08:51.807 - INFO: -----------------------------------------------------------
16:08:51.807 - INFO:   Checking for any auto-failover settings disabled by the SAP HANA Lifecycle Management...
16:08:51.807 - INFO:     Getting nameserver.ini
16:08:51.807 - INFO:       Reading nameserver.ini
16:08:51.807 - INFO:         Layer 'Default' found
16:08:51.809 - INFO:         Layer 'System' found
16:08:51.809 - INFO:   No auto-failover settings disabled by the SAP HANA Lifecycle Management were found.
16:08:51.809 - INFO: -----------------------------------------------------------
16:08:51.809 - INFO: END: Cleaning up configuration changes (start: 16:08:51.807 duration: 00:00:00)
16:08:51.809 - INFO: -----------------------------------------------------------
16:08:51.809 - INFO: Summary of critical errors
16:08:51.789 - ERR :   Renaming SAP HANA instance failed!
16:08:51.789 - ERR :     Renaming master failed!
16:08:51.789 - ERR :       Renaming instance failed

The logs don't show any more. Could it be broken archives or missing files in the archives?

Best regards, 

Sascha

entry.title: Re: My Intro
entry.content:
Hi Aditi, Welcome!

Where are you based in? You can look for your city in Location Groups and join the one that is the closest to you^^

You can also check out Managed Tags and follow the topics that you are interested in. Have fun 🙂

entry.title: Re: Cloud ALM - Business Service Management strategy
entry.content:
Hello Raja,
The Business Service Management will help you to group your systems (and cloud services) into business services which can be understand by the business users.
It provides the possibility to offer a different view apart from the technical landscape definition (like a 4-tier landscape) - to represent business functionality rather then transport routes for example. 
You can find more information on our SAP Cloud ALM Expert Portal for Business Service Management here.
Let me know if this answers your question. 
best regards,
Romy
SAP Support

entry.title: Error with transporting assignment of BP Identification Category to Identification Type
entry.content:
Dear Community,

in our Public Cloud Implementation Project, we would like to assign a customized Business Partner Identification Category to a BP Identification Type.

Identification Categories have been customized. Transport from customizing to test system in public cloud has been done via Export Software Collection. By releasing the software collection in its specific app, the transport is immediately available in the test system and ready to be imported.

Afterwards, we assign the Identification Category to an Identification Type. This setting has to be stored in a customizing transport.  Subsequently, the customizing transport has to be released in order to transport it to the test system. This is not working due to dependencies to the settings, that had to be stored in the software collection.

 

Does anyone of you know how to overcome the issue? A Ticket has already been raised.

 

Many thanks in advance and kind regards

Talke

entry.title: Re: Story Report for Goal Management
entry.content: Hi Punita, It's not clear from your description what you are doing, you could add it as column, or a filter etc. Not clear what your query looks like, which joins are there. I'd recommend you to share more info and some screenshots. Additionally, also check if you have the right permissions to see Goal management with all plans/templates, sometimes the absence of data is caused by permissions

entry.title: Re: Can you tell me the view where I can check the BP Mas...
entry.content: Thank you. I found what I was looking for in I_BusinessPartnerIndustry and I_CreditManagementBP.

entry.title: Re: SAP -Generative AI
entry.content:
Hi @MohanMujumale - Transparency is key when it comes to AI,  I have therefore added the "GenAI Assisted Content" user tag to your post, according to our Rules of Engagement: "If you want to publish content that was helped/created by GenAI, you must add the user tag GenAI Assisted Content. For content where it's not possible to add a user tag (e.g., answers), you must include this statement: "GenAI was used to help generate this content."

The post in its current form is also too short. 
We expect every blog post to consist of a beginning (introduction), a middle (main portion), and an end (a conclusion that wraps everything up). A collection of links or just a video with short text does not qualify as a blog post. You can find more information about writing blog posts in our FAQ.

entry.title: Re: Employment Status in Story reports
entry.content: Hi Punita, Grey "Status" in Basic User Information looks like 100% permission issue. You can check it, if you switch on "Unconditional access to the data" in the Reporting section in your role and then have access to that field in the report, that would mean that you lacked some permission for sure. Second option is to use "Employment status" dimension from Job information for filtering where you need to exclude "Terminated"

entry.title: Re: How do I know if SAP Cash Management is activated?
entry.content:
Hello @Gabor5 ,

Please check out the list below for scope items related to cash management: 


BFABasic Bank Account Management
BFBBasic Cash Operations
J77Advanced Bank Account Management
J78Advanced Cash Operations
2O0Bank Fee Management
2U8Intercompany Process for Cash Concentration
34PTreasury Workstation Cash Integration
3L5 Liquidity Planning

entry.title: Re: Integrating ECC with S/4HANA

(It also depends on the version and type of HANA system: on-premise or cloud, private or public?)

entry.title: Re: Subcontracting with Chargeable Components
entry.content: It has already been confirmed in your other discussion that the said process will work only for one country.  If there is a need for other country, may be you can take the logic from SAP note 599396, test it thoroughly and take the concurrence from the Business Finance team. Needless to mention, some custom development would be required to validate the chargeable value of components being sent.

entry.title: Re: Analytics Cloud & Business ByDesign Business Content...
entry.content:
Hey, all. 

I've managed to solve this problem in the meantime. The issue had to do with our ByDesign configuration.

Steps to fix:
Business Analytics -> Design Reports
Search for relevant ID (e.g. SRMIV_B03_Q0011) and select
Click assign button
Then assign to Corporate Performance work center as highlighted in the screenshot below.

After the changes are saved, the table should now be visible when creating a query (make sure to refresh). Note that the predelivered imports will still provide an error due to a mapping failure. This is easily fixed by just copying the query, running it and then fixing the mapping imports there. Repeat these steps for all of the aforementioned problem tables/queries and the problem should be fixed.

There might be a better or more up to date way to solve this issue. If anyone knows it, please let me know. ByDesign isn't really my field of expertise.

entry.title: Re: CDS Table Entities - for On-Prem & Private Cloud
entry.content: Hi Austin, I am not aware of any information or help docs that SAP will deliver CDS Table entities in a 2023 FPS update.

entry.title: SAP DM website does not open and produce an error: Too many redirects.
entry.content: When we trying to connect to the SAP DM website, we receive the error TOO MANY REDIRECTS. We do not know how to resolve this error. The site worked up to earlier this week.

entry.title: Re: CORUPROC transaction not clearing AFFW data
entry.content:
CORUPROC is a report used to reprocess goods movements that failed due to locking situations during the update process.

  • If you don't analyze and correct errors it will often not be able to correct the errors (only some lock / sequence errors should  be corrected)
  • Did you analyze the dumps (ST22) or error message (SM13), did you then look for OSS notes related to the root causes of dump ?
  • If you don't have performance issues, consider activation of the application log

entry.title: Re: On the Road to SAP BW/4HANA - first stage accomplished
entry.content:
@RolandKramer , First guidance on SAP BW on Hana link is not working, could you please check and re - update.

One of the customer is keen on In-place conversion from SAP BW 7.5 SP 30 on Oracle to SAP BW/4Hana.

As a first step and pre - requisite to move to BW/4Hana, BW should on HANA Database, we are planning for Hana Data base migration. Looking forward for the working link of First Guidance.

entry.title: Re: How to Use 'document-service' API to Upload Documents into Sales & Service Cloud Version 2
entry.content:
Hi @manideepsatya 

Thank you for the blog. In our business scenario, we want to transfer the attachments within Case object to 3rd party system.

Our idea is to perform GET call of a Case --> Fetch the attachment ID --> the use document service to fetch the document details. However, we are stuck here and didn't know how to convert this Json to binary file, so that 3rd party system can receive as actual attachment.

In V1, we can get the binary data of the attachment and then can be converted to file as per the notes - https://me.sap.com/notes/0003371608

Can you guide us here how to transfer the attachment to the other system?

I am using userexit USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE and I update table XVBAP[] (field ROUTE)

Everything is working fine but the schedule line dates are not updated !

 

If it's done manually :

 

 

How can I update schedule line dates programatically ?

entry.title: Re: unable to print preview pdf for PO
entry.content: Please check SAP notes or contact SAP Support.

entry.title: Re: Can create the new Exchange Rate Type
entry.content:
Sawatdee khrap Chanaporn,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you cannot create a completely new exchange rate type.

 

Key Points:

  • Only predefined exchange rate types (like M, P, etc.) are allowed in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
  • These rate types are delivered by SAP and cannot be extended or customized to add new ones.
  • However, you can use the existing exchange rate types and assign different currency pairs, validity periods, and rates using the available configuration and apps.

 

Workaround options:

If your business needs different exchange rates for various purposes (e.g., planning vs. accounting), consider:

  • Using a different standard rate type (e.g., use M for accounting and P for planning).
  • Uploading rates regularly via apps like:
    • "Manage Exchange Rates"
    • "Import Exchange Rates"
    • Or by integration with SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) or an external market data provider.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: How to maintain the SOBES field for specific combinat...
entry.content:
The domain values/conversion of this field are maintained in the domain and table T460B, which has the following status: S – system table, edited only by SAP; change – modification.

(There are effectively no value 5 in my systems either. What would this value be used for, in which industry solution ?)

entry.title: Re: Error "Rule Service with given ID does not exist" when Consuming Decision Table API(SB
entry.content: Having the same issue right now - did you resolve it?

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Google Chart Does Not Render Any More
entry.content:
Hi,

SAP Build Apps has stricter security and rendering sandboxing compared to AppGyver. D3.js or Google Charts may not render properly due to missing permissions or incompatible WebView behavior. Try embedding the chart in an iframe or use a custom WebView component. Also, ensure your scripts are loaded securely (HTTPS, CORS-compliant) and are not blocked by SAP Build’s runtime.

Feel free to check erproots.com (please apply a nofollow attribute if referencing).

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: Can create the new Exchange Rate Type
entry.content:
Hi @Jeremy_Deo,

 I have test creating the new exchange rate type, and I can use it for recording Billing documents in the system.

If I manually create a new exchange rate type, will the configuration be lost after a system update release?

Please confirm.

Thanks,

Chanaporn S.

entry.title: Re: unable to see in HTML5 Application in SAP BTP COCKPIT...
entry.content:
I created a new MTA with app, srv , db  and deployed to CF and can see the app in html5 app repository and even successfully added the same to launchpad site space and page

When i display the data locally in CAPM service using "cds w" it is allowing me to get(display) and post the data in the sandbox fiori.
But when I try to get the data by selecting the html5 app or directly from launchpad, getting internal server error (500) for metadata and service.

 

all my Xsapp.json , xs-security.json , mta.yaml and manifest. Json are configured for xsuaa token exchange with added role collection in BTP.

can anyone help on this ?

entry.title: Re: Error "Rule Service with given ID does not exist" whe...
entry.content:
Hi,

This error typically means the deployed rule service (decision table) wasn't activated or the correct rule service ID wasn’t used in your Postman request. Double-check the following:

  1. Ensure the decision table is deployed successfully in the Build Process Automation Monitor.

  2. Use the correct Rule Service ID from the Monitor → Rule Service Details screen.

  3. Make sure you’re using the right tenant base URL and authorization token in Postman.

If recently deployed, try redeploying and regenerating the trigger—then test again.

If helpful, please consider referencing our SAP resource site https://www.erproots.com with a no-follow link – thank you!

entry.title: Re: PPF message not always processed, stucked in SPPFP (S...
entry.content: We encounter the same Problem. My assumption was the number of dialogue work processes too low. But not sure. We are changing our program to post GI delivery by delivery not all of them in one go. Let's see whether it helps.

entry.title: Re: limitations of SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) when it come...
entry.content:
Hi,

  • Cell-Level Calculations: SAC has limited cell-level formula support. Unlike Webi’s flexible cell-specific logic, SAC uses model-based calculations and restricted formulas within tables or charts.

  • Webi Functions Equivalents:

    • Force Merge/Merge Dimensions: SAC doesn’t have a direct merge dimension feature. You need to model data properly beforehand or use linked models/blended data.

    • Where Conditions: You can simulate this using restricted measures or filters, though it’s less dynamic than Webi’s "Where" clause.

  • Publication Formats: SAC supports exporting to PDF and Excel. HTML export/publication is not natively supported like in Webi.

  • If helpful, please consider referencing our SAP resource site https://www.erproots.com with a no-follow link – thank you!

entry.title: Re: How to Link a Public Dimension to another dimension i...
entry.content:
@Huzaifa2 I am not sure what exactly does linking your dimension to public dimension means here. The only link you can establish between 2 dimension is by using attribute where in one dimension members are attributes in other dimension. Even this relationship has to be done manually.

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Error "Rule Service with given ID does not exist" whe...
entry.content: in my case, the Rule Service was configured within the Process Automation, so we cannot deploy / redeploy it manually. Do you have any recommendations on this?

entry.title: Has someone any idea how can I write this complex Query in sql-anywhere?
entry.content:
For some reason, I need a query that builds all the input parameters of a given procedure along with their values in one string, the query should work with any procedure regardless of the number of the input parameters.

For example I have such a procedure:

create or replace procedure mytest (par1 varchar(10) , par2 varchar(10), par3 int)
result (ss long varchar)
begin
select 'how can i write this?'
end;

And my call looks like this:

select * from mytest('abc', null, 3)

 

Then I expect the result like this:

par1='abc', par2=null, par3=3

entry.title: Re: unable to see in HTML5 Application in SAP BTP COCKPIT...
entry.content:
Hi,

If cf html5-list shows the app but it's not visible in BTP Cockpit's HTML5 Applications, try the following:

  1. Check the managed router settings: Make sure your xs-app.json and mta.yaml files include the correct managed-app-router config.

  2. Enable HTML5 Dynamic Destinations: Go to your subaccount - HTML5 Applications - Settings - ensure “Enable HTML5 Dynamic Destinations” is turned on.

  3. Verify App Deployment: Ensure the app module is properly assigned to the html5-apps-repo service.

  4. Refresh after some time: In Trial accounts, HTML5 apps can take a few minutes to appear due to backend sync.

If the issue persists, redeploy using: cf html5-pushand clear your browser cache.

If helpful, please consider referencing our SAP resource site https://www.erproots.com with a no-follow link – thank you!

entry.title: Re: Build Process Automation fails only to send emails wi...
entry.content:
Self resolved.

https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/configuring-smtp-mail-destination?locale=en-US

The above help mentioned...

*Note
"If you have been using bpmworkflowruntime_mail, bpmworkflowruntime_mail takes precedence over sap_process_automation_mail. consider merging the two destinations into one destination. For example, if both destinations have the same properties and the same configuration, you can remove the “bpmworkflowruntime_mail”.


When running test mail on Control Tower, the sap_process_automation_mail destination is used with the correct credentials, but when mail is sent in a workflow, bpmworkflowruntime_mail is used first bpmworkflowruntime_mail
If the bpmworkflowruntime_mail credentials are invalid, an authentication error will occur.
Therefore, after changing the various property settings of bpmworkflowruntime_mail to the same values as sap_process_automation_mail, the mail was successfully sent by executing mail sending on the process again.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Enable Joule for on-prem ABAP Development
entry.content: When will Joule code assistant be made available in the future for an on-premise S/4 HANA systems. And if not what is the rationale for leaving on premise out

entry.title: Re: How to Use 'document-service' API to Upload Documents into Sales & Service Cloud Version 2
entry.content:
@Manson_J 

Once you retervie from Attachment Id ... you can send it to external system as we have been doing. 

This blog may provide you an hint as i hope you might need to understand the Attachment Id which will contain Base64encoded of the document which you need to decode it and extract it in pdf format.

https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-blog-posts-by-sap/how-to-upload-files-to-document-service-and-link-attachments-to-leads-or/ba-p/14023039

entry.title: Re: Session on Input Service Distribution (ISD) functionality in SAP
entry.content:
Dear Team,

Kindly guide me on ISD GSTR6 process.

I booked document with ISD business place, then that is successfully got distributed in tcode J1IG_ISDN and created separate documents with respective receiving business place were crated.

Now my query is, whether the new documents created should be uploaded to DCS or not?

Thanks for support.

entry.title: Re: Extensibility Approaches in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
@Harish_C 

Can you create a custom Business Catalog in SAP Public Cloud?

Not via standard UI—only possible through Developer Extensibility in ABAP.             

Can you use it in Custom Catalog Extension by creating an application?

  • If you already have a catalog (via developer), you can indeed add your app through Custom Catalog Extensions at key‑user level.

  • But from just the UI alone, you can’t create a brand-new catalog—you can only extend existing ones with your app.

If you need a new business catalog to hold custom apps (not just extend existing ones), you must use Developer Extensibility (via ABAP in Eclipse or ADT). This allows you to:

  • Create a Custom Business Catalog object in the ABAP repository.

  • Add your custom apps via target mappings, and then use that catalog in a business role

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/create-business-catalog-to-grant-access-to-custom-business-object/qaq-p/14050193?utm_source=chatgpt.com

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2633830?utm_source=chatgpt.com

entry.title: Re: How to add data (z table) in SAP Public cloud Customi...
entry.content:
You cannot create traditional Z‑tables via IMG/customizing in the public cloud.

Instead, you define Custom Business Objects through key‑user apps (no-code), which create tables and allow data entry and OData exposure.

If you need developer-level control, use ABAP Cloud to create database tables, CDS views, behavior definitions, and OData services—giving full CRUD and integration capabilities.

entry.title: Event Management Queue Health and Performance Monitoring
entry.content:
Monitoring SAP Event Management Extraction Queues

In previous cases, SAP Event Management (EM) extraction queues in application systems have experienced sudden growth. These queues refer to RFC outbound queues in any application system connected to an SAP EM system (e.g., "EM_TMS_TOR", "EM_ESC_DELIV"), which can be viewed in transaction SMQ1.

Problem Overview:

When this growth occurs, the queue may still appear to be running without errors, and this can lead to confusion. Customers might assume an issue has occurred and contact SAP Support to investigate the sudden growth. In most cases, however, this issue arises when a queue reaches a saturation point: more new entries are created than are processed and removed from the queue.

Two key factors contribute to this situation:

  1. The number of new entries created (related to application object changes and event messages sent).
  2. The processing time for entries in the SAP EM system (the RFC destination system).

Unfortunately, these variables are usually not visible to users and administrators, and they may not be aware of the queue approaching saturation.

Solution Overview:

To help administrators monitor queue health and detect when a queue is approaching saturation, we’ve introduced a development that provides visibility into the number of calls to the SAP EM system and into the number of processed objects and the time required for processing queue entries in the SAP EM system.

This solution is delivered via two SAP Notes:

  • SAP Note 3516253: To be implemented in the application system (where the outbound queues reside).
  • SAP Note 3513421: To be implemented in the SAP EM system.

Important: These notes cannot be implemented manually or through the SAP Note Assistant. You must implement the referenced Support Packages.

 

Key Features of the SAP Notes:

SAP Note 3516253 - Queue Health Monitoring in the Application System:

This note introduces the ability to monitor the "health" of EM extraction queues. Health, in this context, refers to the ratio of idle versus busy times for a queue.

  • Transaction /SAPTRX/MONQH: Used to monitor queue health.

    • Queue Name: The name of the queue.
    • RFC Destination: The destination system for RFC communication (note that it is case-sensitive).
    • No. of days the job runs: Specifies the number of days to monitor.
    • Sample Interval (seconds): Determines how often the queue status is checked.

Stopping an active monitoring process is done within the same transaction (use the inactive button ("Stop monitoring process") as shown in the interface).

 

SAP Note 3513421 - Evaluation in SAP EM System:

This note provides evaluation functions within the SAP EM system and includes start/stop functionality for BAPI performance monitoring.

  • Transaction /SAPTRX/EVALQH: Used to evaluate monitoring data from the application system.

    • Application System: The system configured in your SAP EM customizing (configured via transaction SPRO → SAP Reference IMG → Event Management → General Settings → Define Application System).
    • Queue Name and RFC Destination: Correspond to the parameters used in the /SAPTRX/MONQH transaction (again: RFC Destination is case-sensitive!).
    • Start/End Date: Defines the subset of the monitoring data you are evaluating.
    • Idle% Threshold: Defines the minimum percentage of idle time to consider the queue "healthy." The default might fit, but you can adapt it if you either would like to be alerted early or if you want to prevent small spikes to raise alarms.
    • Sample% Threshold: Used to exclude days with insufficient sample data.

After executing the evaluation, you can view the results:

  • Idle Counter: Counts the number of times the queue has been idle.
  • Busy Counter: Counts the number of times the queue has been busy (including error statuses).
  • Idle Percentage: Shows the ratio of idle to total samples.
  • Sample Percentage: Displays the number of samples taken compared to the maximum number of samples within the evaluation period.

Red fields indicate values lower than the Idle% Threshold. Grayed out fields relate to an insufficient number of samples, compared to the Sample% Threshold.

Data can be exported to a spreadsheet for further analysis or graphical representation.

You can schedule /SAPTRX/EVALQH to run periodically in the background. If any Idle Percentage falls below the set threshold, the job will be marked as "error status," enabling automatic monitoring for potential issues.

 

BAPI Performance Monitoring:

To gain further insights into queue health, the BAPI Performance Monitoring function can be used. This feature makes previously hidden variables visible, helping administrators perform root cause analysis if queue health deteriorates.

  • Report /SAPTRX/ACT_PMON: Starts/stops BAPI performance monitoring.

    • The system records the number of objects processed and the time required for each BAPI call (/SAPTRX/BAPI_EH_POST and /SAPTRX/BAPI_EH_ADDEVENTMSG_02), as well as additional information about the process type and the source of the call.
    • Monitoring provides insight into performance, helping to identify bottlenecks.

  • Report /SAPTRX/EVAL_PMON: Evaluates BAPI performance data.

    • You can filter data by BAPI Type, Origin/Sender, and Process Type.
    • The Threshold time per object (ms) allows you to define acceptable processing times and highlight underperforming calls. With that parameter you can easily spot low performance in the resulting table:

 

Technical Details:

Queue Health Monitoring (Application System):

  • The monitoring process is implemented as asynchronous RFC, running at intervals defined by the Sample interval parameter.
  • This process is lightweight and should not impact system performance, even when monitoring multiple queues.
  • To check the status of the monitoring process, you can use transaction /SAPTRX/MONQH or look for locks on table /SAPTRX/MONQH (via transaction SM21).
  • The user starting a monitoring process needs the authorization for transaction SMQ1. The RFC user reading the monitoring data (the evaluation program in the SAP EM system is triggering the RFC) also needs this authorization.

BAPI Performance Monitoring:

  • Monitoring is based on calls to the /SAPTRX/BAPI_EH_POST and /SAPTRX/BAPI_EH_ADDEVENTMSG_02 BAPIs. Only runtime data for successful executions will be recorded.
  • No DB locking issues are expected, and the impact on system performance is minimal.
  • An "empty" BAPI call (e. g. created with the test function of transaction SE37 or with custom code) has zero objects, but for the "Time per Object" calculation it will be treated as having one object.
  • There currently is no support for the way an SAP Object Event Repository implementation (SAP EM 9.20 only) will affect the number of objects in a BAPI call. If you are using SAP OER it is recommended to filter out the related Process Types in the evaluation report.
  • The processing of repost data from the tables /SAPTRX/RP_* or of event messages from table /SAPTRX/LOCKEDEH (see SAP Note 656990) will not be monitored. The assumption is that any performance changes regarding BAPI executions will affect the processing from those sources in the exact same way.

The volume of the DB tables used for both, queue health monitoring in the application system and BAPI performance monitoring in the SAP EM system, is supposed to stay negligible, due to the aggregation of data on day level.

Irregular Monitoring Cancellations:

  • The monitoring process may be cancelled due to system conditions (e.g., application server restart, resource issues). If this occurs, you can use the /SAPTRX/MON_QH_RESTARTER program to restart the monitoring process automatically.
  • The program will attempt to resume monitoring based on the remaining days, though precision of the scheduling is limited to one day (-> you might monitor slightly longer than originally intended).
  • If there are frequent cancellations, /SAPTRX/MON_QH_RESTARTER should be scheduled as a periodic job.

 

Conclusion:

These developments allow you to monitor the health of EM extraction queues and evaluate the performance of BAPI calls, providing greater visibility into the system. By implementing the recommended SAP Notes (or rather the referenced Support Packages) and using the associated transactions, administrators can proactively manage system performance, identify bottlenecks, and maintain optimal queue health.

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting: Why are the elimination journals rev...
entry.content:
When setting up a reclassification method, Periodic Reclassification means you upload data for each individual period. In this mode, you don’t need auto-reversal, because each period stands alone.

However, if Periodic Reclassification is not activated, you’re importing Year‑To‑Date (YTD) accumulated data. Every time you upload this YTD data, the system needs to reverse the previous elimination entries—otherwise, you’ll double-count prior eliminations.

Example:

  • Period 1: You upload data = 100,000, and eliminate intercompany transactions amounting to 5,000.

  • Period 2: You now upload YTD data = 120,000, with intercompany eliminations totaling 7,000.

Without reversing the original 5,000 elimination from Period 1, the system would deduct 5,000 + 7,000 = 12,000, which skews your consolidated totals. You must reverse the prior elimination (i.e., add back the 5,000) before applying the new elimination of 7,000.

entry.title: Re: Import deployed code from cloud foundry to BAS
entry.content:
Use terminal

cf login <endpoint>

cf html5-get <app-name>

it would be imported to you current folder

 

Thanks,

Jagadeep

entry.content:
Really well-written! While most people focus on the technical implementation of enterprise mobility, I’ve seen an interesting take on how user adoption and training can significantly impact the success of an enterprise mobility solution in this guide: https://mobisoftinfotech.com/services/enterprise-mobility .
Curious to hear what you think about this approach—especially when it comes to scaling mobility across large, non-tech-savvy teams.

entry.title: Re: SAP Digital Manufactoring: Partial Confirmation possi...
entry.content: Hi, assuming you are talking about Discrete execution in Work Center / Operation Activity PODs, see Handling SFCs with Lot Size > 1 in SAP Digital Manufacturing. Br, Manoel

entry.title: Re: Error with transporting assignment of BP Identificati...
entry.content:
Hi Talke,

Thanks for the detailed description, this is a known limitation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, where you’re working with two different transport mechanisms:

  • Export Software Collection (for in-app extensibility, e.g. ID categories)
  • Customizing Requests (for SSCUI/customizing activities like assigning the category to the ID type)

You’ve correctly customized and transported the Identification Category using a Software Collection, but when assigning it to an Identification Type via a customizing activity, the system blocks the transport because the referenced object (the category) was moved via Software Collection and hasn’t been "recognized" in the customizing layer yet.

Common workaround:

You typically need to ensure the imported software collection is activated first in the target system (test) before the customizing transport is released and imported.

Steps

  1. Go to “Import Software Collection” app in the target/test system.
  2. Import and activate the software collection containing the new Identification Category.
  3. Wait until the status is fully “active.”
  4. Only then go back and release/import the customizing request (with the assignment step).
  5. If still blocked, re-open the customizing activity, reassign the category (now known to the system), and create a fresh customizing transport.

 

Please note

  • This mismatch is a known transport sequencing issue concerning extensibility (In-App vs SSCUI).
  • You’ve done the right thing by raising a ticket — SAP Support sometimes needs to assist by resetting an incorrect status flag or dependency.

References

SAP doesn’t yet provide a unified transport mechanism between these two layers in Public Cloud. A helpful SAP Help reference on this topic is:

  • SAP Help Portal 

How to Manage and Export Your Software Collection 

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: How to Migrate Stock from IM Managed to EWM Managed S...
entry.content:
Hi Chuma,

Thank you for your reference

entry.title: Re: S4 Readiness Checker report out of BW7.5 System - For...

entry.title: Re: SAP DM: Integration Scenario with Stock Room Management?
entry.content:
Hi,

I suggest always checking SAP Help and Roadmap for product information.

DM/EWM integration is available for a long time now, see Integration with EWM.

For WM, you must consider developing custom integrations.

Br, Manoel

entry.title: A Simpler Way to Manage Task Relations in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Let’s be honest, when you’re deep into task management in SAP Cloud ALM, the last thing you want is to go on a tab-hunting mission.

If you’ve worked with tasks that had multiple associations like test cases, documents, requirements, and so on—you probably know what I’m talking about. Until recently, each of these lived in its own separate tab on the task type detail page. And while that may have seemed like a good idea at first, it came with a few annoying side effects:

  • Too many tabs cluttering the screen
  • Hard to find the right one (especially on smaller displays)
  • Just plain overwhelming when you're in the middle of getting work done

 

So... What’s Changed?

Now, all those scattered tabs are gone. 
Instead, we’ve introduced a much cleaner and smarter way to see everything that’s related to a task, all in one place.

 

 

Say Hello to the Relations Tab 

This new tab replaces all those individual relation tabs and brings everything together in a neat, organized view.

Here’s how it works:

  • Everything’s in one tab: No more switching between different tabs. Just go to Relations.
  • Dropdown for related items: You’ll see all associated items—like test cases, documents, features, etc.—as a simple dropdown list.
  • Click to go directly to what you need: Select an item and jump straight to the related object.
  • Counts included: Next to each item, you’ll see how many are linked. For example: “5 Test Cases”, “2 Documents”, “1 Feature”.

 

 

What’s Not in the Relations Tab

To keep things focused and uncluttered, only relationship-based content appears in the Relations tab. Some task-specific details still live outside of it and for good reason.

Here’s what you’ll still find in their dedicated sections:

  • Description: The main summary or details about the task stay at the top of the task page.
  • Accelerators and References: These assets are still available in their own section to keep them easy to find and separate from task-to-task relationships.
  • Additional Information: Any extra contextual info that supports the task but isn’t a direct relationship is also kept outside the Relations tab.

This approach keeps the Relations tab clean and focused only on navigable, connected objects so you get exactly what you need without the noise.

 

Why This Is a Big Deal (Even Though It Looks Small)

On the surface, this might feel like a minor UI tweak, But trust me, it makes a big difference when you're working day-to-day. Here’s why:

  • It’s way easier to use - Everything is right where you need it. No guessing, no tab-hopping.
  • You save time - Click less, scroll less, and get to the info faster.
  • It’s less overwhelming - With a single, focused view, it’s easier to see the full picture without being buried in tabs.
  • Works great on smaller screens - If you're working on a laptop or tablet, the new design feels much more manageable and clean.
  • Makes onboarding smoother - If you’re new to SAP Cloud ALM (or helping someone who is), this change makes it much easier to learn and navigate.

 

Final Thoughts

This update may not shout for attention, but that’s kind of the point. It quietly improves your experience and makes task management a little more intuitive, a little less frustrating, and a whole lot more efficient.

So next time you open a task, check out the new Relations tab. We think you’ll like how much simpler things feel.

And as always, we love hearing your feedback. it’s what helps us make changes like this in the first place.

entry.title: CAP Application not mocking remote services on every second app start
entry.content:
Hello,

we are currently facing the issue accross multiple CAP projects, that during local development, mocking of remote services doesnt work as expected. 

Every second time our app restarts in local development during cds watch the remote mock services are not started.

We have observed that behavior during in multiple projects:
- It seems only to happen when trying to mock multiple remote services at the same time (3+)
- It seems to only occur in CAP node.js not Java
- It seems to be related to the cds.env not being populated properly.

We have first noted it down since cds 8.3.0

We can mitigate the problem by either causing the server to restart again or running the mocks on a different server seperatly, but a solution would be appreciated.

Best regards
Sören

entry.title: Re: unable to print preview pdf for PO
entry.content: I don't understand what you mean. Here is where to contact SAP Support: https://me.sap.com/home.

entry.title: Deploying a Fiori Adaption Project application to the Fiori launchpad in public cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

for a customer project, we need to modify a standard fiori application (doubtful account validation), and deploy it to the public cloud. Currently i try to deploy the application without changes, because i want to have a working deployment process, before i change things in the app.

For the deployment i followed a tutorial that seems to fit my needs:
Link 

My deployment settings in BAS look different. So i was not able to adjust everything what was mentioned in the tutorial.

When i try to select the app in the PC in the "manage launchpad space" transaction, i simply cannot select it.

Has anyone experience in doing adaption projects and public cloud deployments?

Thanks in advance !

entry.title: Re: Actions in Behavior Definition For CDS Custom Entity
entry.content:
Hi,

This code didn’t work 😞

My questions;

After clicking the action button, the custom CDS data source class is called again. Why does this happen? I only want to update the status icon on the screen after the action..

Do you have any example or suggestion for this?

unmanaged implementation in class zbp_c_rap_exchange_rate_tcmb unique;
//strict;

define behavior for zc_rap_exchange_rate_tcmb alias exchange
lock master
//authorization master ( global, instance )
{

  field ( readonly ) ForexType, SapDate, CurrencyCode;

  action ( features : instance ) SetExchange result[1] $self ;

  mapping for zc_rap_exchange_rate_tcmb control zexchange_rate_tcmb_s
  {
      ForexType    = ForexType;
      SapDate      = SapDate;
      CurrencyCode = CurrencyCode;
      SapDate_UI   = SapDate_UI;
      Isim         = Isim;
      StatusCode   = StatusCode;
      StatusIcon   = StatusIcon;
      Unit         = Unit;
      ForexBuying  = ForexBuying;
      ForexSelling = ForexSelling;
      ApiDate      = ApiDate;
  }

}

 

CLASS lhc_exchange DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_abap_behv_message.

  PRIVATE SECTION.

    METHODS get_instance_features FOR INSTANCE FEATURES
      IMPORTING keys REQUEST requested_features FOR exchange RESULT result.

    METHODS read FOR READ
      IMPORTING keys FOR READ exchange RESULT result.

    METHODS lock FOR LOCK
      IMPORTING keys FOR LOCK exchange.

    METHODS SetExchange FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING keys FOR ACTION exchange~SetExchange RESULT result.

    METHODS:
      _read_exchange_rate
        IMPORTING
          !im_kurst       TYPE I_ExchangeRateRawData-ExchangeRateType DEFAULT 'M'
          !im_fcurr       TYPE I_ExchangeRateRawData-SourceCurrency
          !im_tcurr       TYPE I_ExchangeRateRawData-TargetCurrency
          !im_gdatu       TYPE I_ExchangeRateRawData-ValidityStartDate
        EXPORTING
          es_erate        TYPE I_ExchangeRateRawData
        RETURNING
          VALUE(sy_subrc) TYPE int1.


ENDCLASS.

CLASS lhc_exchange IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD SetExchange.

    DATA: _failed   TYPE RESPONSE FOR FAILED zc_rap_exchange_rate_tcmb,
          _reported TYPE RESPONSE FOR REPORTED zc_rap_exchange_rate_tcmb.

    " Data structures for Z-table
    DATA: ztable_data TYPE TABLE OF zrap_tcmb_log.

    " Key structure matching entity primary keys
    TYPES: BEGIN OF ts_entity_key,
             ForexType    TYPE c LENGTH 1,
             SapDate      TYPE d,
             CurrencyCode TYPE c LENGTH 5,
           END OF ts_entity_key.

    " Tables for fetching process
    DATA: keys_for_fetch TYPE TABLE OF ts_entity_key,
          full_data      TYPE TABLE OF zrap_tcmb_etab.


    " Extract keys from selected entries
    LOOP AT keys ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<key>).
      APPEND VALUE #(
        ForexType    = <key>-%key-ForexType
        SapDate      = <key>-%key-SapDate
        CurrencyCode = <key>-%key-CurrencyCode
      ) TO keys_for_fetch.
    ENDLOOP.

    IF keys_for_fetch IS NOT INITIAL.

      TRY.
          SELECT * FROM zrap_tcmb_etab
            FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @Keys_for_fetch
            WHERE SapDate      = @Keys_for_fetch-SapDate AND
                  CurrencyCode = @Keys_for_fetch-CurrencyCode
                  INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @full_data.

        CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(error).
          " Handle fetch errors
          LOOP AT keys ASSIGNING <key>.
            INSERT VALUE #( %tky = <key>-%tky ) INTO TABLE failed-exchange.
            INSERT VALUE #( %tky = <key>-%tky
                            %msg = new_message_with_text( severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
                                                          text     = 'FETCH_ERROR' ) ) INTO TABLE _reported-exchange.
          ENDLOOP.
          failed   = _failed.
          reported = _reported.
          RETURN.
      ENDTRY.
    ENDIF.

    " Process data and prepare Z-table records and UI updates
    LOOP AT keys ASSIGNING <key>.
      READ TABLE full_data ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<entity>)
        WITH KEY SapDate      = <key>-%key-SapDate
                 CurrencyCode = <key>-%key-CurrencyCode.

      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        " Prepare Z-table entry with key and non-key fields
        APPEND VALUE #(
          forextype    = <key>-%key-ForexType
          sapdate      = <key>-%key-SapDate
          currencycode = <key>-%key-CurrencyCode
          isim          = <entity>-Isim
          sapdate_ui    = <entity>-sapdate
          processed_on  = sy-datum
          processed_by  = sy-uname
          status        = 'COMPLETED'
        ) TO ztable_data.

        " Prepare UI update data
        APPEND VALUE #(
          %tky  = <key>-%tky
          %param = VALUE #( BASE CORRESPONDING #( <entity> ) "%data not found 
                    StatusCode = '1'
                    StatusIcon = 'sap-icon://accept'
                  )
        ) TO result.
      ELSE.
        " Handle missing data
        INSERT VALUE #( %tky = <key>-%tky ) INTO TABLE failed-exchange.
        INSERT VALUE #( %tky = <key>-%tky
                        %msg = new_message_with_text(
                                 severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
                                 text     = 'FETCH_ERROR: Entity data not found' )
                      ) INTO TABLE _reported-exchange.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.

    " Save data to Z-table
    IF ztable_data IS NOT INITIAL.
      TRY.
          MODIFY zrap_tcmb_log FROM TABLE @ztable_data.
          IF sy-subrc <> 0.
            " Handle database insert errors
            CLEAR result.
            LOOP AT keys ASSIGNING <key>.
              INSERT VALUE #( %tky = <key>-%tky ) INTO TABLE failed-exchange.
              INSERT VALUE #( %tky = <key>-%tky
                              %msg = new_message_with_text(
                                       severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
                                       text     = 'DB_ERROR: Failed to save to database' )
                            ) INTO TABLE _reported-exchange.
            ENDLOOP.
          ENDIF.
        CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(db_error).
          " Handle database exceptions
          CLEAR result.
          LOOP AT keys ASSIGNING <key>.
            INSERT VALUE #( %tky = <key>-%tky ) INTO TABLE failed-exchange.
            INSERT VALUE #( %tky = <key>-%tky
                            %msg = new_message_with_text(
                                     severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
                                     text     = |EXCEPTION: { db_error->get_text( ) }| )
                          ) INTO TABLE _reported-exchange.
          ENDLOOP.
      ENDTRY.
    ENDIF.

    failed   = _failed.
    reported = _reported.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_instance_features.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD read.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD lock.


  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD _read_exchange_rate.

    sy_subrc = 0.
    SELECT SINGLE *
             FROM I_ExchangeRateRawData
             WHERE ExchangeRateType  EQ @IM_kurst AND
                   SourceCurrency    EQ @IM_fcurr AND
                   TargetCurrency    EQ @IM_tcurr AND
                   ValidityStartDate EQ @IM_gdatu
                   INTO @ES_erate.
    IF sy-subrc NE 0.
      sy_subrc = 4.
    ENDIF.

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS lsc_ZC_RAP_EXCHANGE_RATE_TCMB DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_saver.
  PROTECTED SECTION.

    METHODS finalize REDEFINITION.

    METHODS check_before_save REDEFINITION.

    METHODS save REDEFINITION.

    METHODS cleanup REDEFINITION.

    METHODS cleanup_finalize REDEFINITION.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS lsc_ZC_RAP_EXCHANGE_RATE_TCMB IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD finalize.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD check_before_save.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD save.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD cleanup.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD cleanup_finalize.
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Re: S4 Readiness Checker report out of BW7.5 System - For...
entry.content:
Hi Roland

We are going to with BW4 HANA as apart of the S4 Upgrade. The readiness checks were run as part of this process of moving towards BW4 HANA

entry.title: Revenue & COGS elimination in Group Reporting
entry.content:
When using the Consolidation Monitor to eliminate revenue against COGS, we follow two sequences:

  1. Sequence 010 – Eliminate revenue using a clearing account

  2. Sequence 020 – Eliminate COGS using the same clearing account

My question:
If there's one revenue financial statement item but multiple related COGS accounts, how do we handle the elimination in Sequence 020? Specifically, how should we distribute the revenue elimination across the various COGS accounts linked to that revenue item?

entry.title: Re: How to convert Hierarchy to Attribute in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello @Masaaki,

We have same requirement for GL Account. But in our scenario, we don't have fixed hierarchy levels.

If i understand correctly from what you have mentioned levels should be fixed as pre-requisite.

Any other way to achieve this when levels are not fixed.

Regards,

Dhruv

entry.title: Re: Enable Multi-Input Field on the Object Page using RAP
entry.content:
Hi,

I think the best way is to implement a validation for this example.

Best wishes,

Ramjee Korada

entry.title: CONNECT S4HANA TO TALLY
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have a client which have around 20 entities in Tally, we want to bring that data from tally to S/4 HANA, data transfer should be seamless without any manual intervention.

Is there any way to do it.

Manual data load is not an option it should be automatic data transfer.

Looking forward to valuable Suggestions ?

Thanks and Regards,

Mayank Gupta

entry.title: TAX Invoice Tax Amount Tolerance Setting
entry.content:
TAX Invoice Tolerance Setting Solution (Business Logic)
1. Background and Reiteration of Requirements
In the Concur to SAP system integration project, the finance department faces a core
challenge: how to intelligently handle discrepancies in GST (Goods and Services Tax)
amounts on New Zealand supplier invoices. The specific requirements are:
Allow: For invoices with a large number of line items (e.g., 200 lines), small
discrepancies (e.g., an accumulation of 0.01 difference per line, potentially totaling
up to 2.00) due to rounding of each line's GST calculation or other rounding rules
(e.g., round up, round down). Such discrepancies are considered reasonable and
should be allowed for posting.
Block: For manually issued single-line invoices, if a similar discrepancy of 2.00
occurs, it is considered an unreasonable calculation error and should be blocked
from posting, requiring the supplier to provide a new invoice.
The key is to design a tolerance rule that can differentiate between these two scenarios,
rather than simply setting a fixed amount or percentage tolerance.

2. In-depth Analysis of Invoice Generation Logic and its
Impact on GST Discrepancies
The supplier's invoicing system or manual calculation method is the fundamental cause
of GST discrepancies. We need to understand the following two main invoice generation
logics and their impact on GST discrepancies:
2.1. Scenario 1: GST Calculated Line by Line and then Summarized
Calculation Process: The supplier's system calculates GST for each non-taxable
line item amount separately (non-taxable amount × 15%), then rounds the GST
amount for each line item (e.g., round half up, round up, or round down). Finally,
all rounded GST amounts from all line items are accumulated to get the total GST
amount for the invoice.
Source of Discrepancy: Since each line item's GST calculation may have a tiny
rounding error (e.g., actual GST is 0.154, rounded to 0.15; actual GST is 0.156,
rounded to 0.16), when there are many line items, these small rounding errors can
accumulate. As per your description, each line might generate a difference of up to
0.01, so 200 lines could accumulate a total GST discrepancy of up to 200 * 0.01 =
2.00 compared to the system's precise calculation.
Characteristics: This type of discrepancy is expected and reasonable, resulting
from the inherent characteristics of computer systems handling floating-point
numbers and rounding rules. The discrepancy amount is usually positively
correlated with the number of line items.
2.2. Scenario 2: GST Calculated on the Total Non-Taxable Amount
Calculation Process: The supplier's system first sums up all non-taxable line item
amounts to get the total non-taxable amount for the invoice. Then, GST is
calculated uniformly on this total non-taxable amount (total non-taxable amount
× 15%), and a single rounding is performed to get the total GST amount for the
invoice.
Source of Discrepancy: In this case, rounding occurs only once, after the total
amount's GST calculation. Therefore, theoretically, the discrepancy between the
total GST amount and the system's precise calculation should be very small,
usually not exceeding 0.01 (depending on the rounding rule).
Characteristics: If such an invoice shows a large discrepancy like 2.00, it is highly
unreasonable. This usually means there is an issue with the supplier's calculation
logic or a manual entry error, rather than simple accumulated rounding.
2.3. Impact of Rounding Rules
Different rounding rules (round half up, round up, round down) affect the specific
rounding results during single-line or total amount calculations, but their core impact is:
Round Half Up/To Nearest Even: This is the most common rounding method, with
discrepancies typically between +/- 0.005.
Round Up: Always rounds towards positive infinity, e.g., 0.151 -> 0.16. This may
result in the total GST being slightly higher than the precise value.
Round Down: Always rounds towards negative infinity, e.g., 0.159 -> 0.15. This may
result in the total GST being slightly lower than the precise value.
Regardless of the rounding rule used, for single-line or a small number of line items, the
resulting discrepancy should be within a very small range (usually less than 0.01). Only
when multiple line items accumulate can these tiny discrepancies add up to a relatively
larger amount.

3. Tolerance Rule Design (Business Logic)
Based on the above analysis, we cannot simply set a fixed amount or percentage
tolerance. We need a dynamic tolerance model based on the number of invoice line
items and expected accumulated errors.
3.1. Core Idea: Expected Accumulated Error Model
We assume that the maximum reasonable rounding error for each line's GST calculation
is Max_Line_Error (based on your description, this can be set to 0.01). Then, for an
invoice with N line items, the reasonable accumulated error range for its total GST
amount should be:
Expected_Tolerance = N * Max_Line_Error
For example: * 1-line invoice: Expected_Tolerance = 1 * 0.01 = 0.01 * 200-line
invoice: Expected_Tolerance = 200 * 0.01 = 2.00
3.2. Proposed Tolerance Rule
We recommend adopting the following two-tier tolerance validation logic:
Tier 1: Absolute Fixed Tolerance (for all invoices)
Set a very small absolute tolerance, such as 0.05 or 0.10. This tolerance is
used to capture extremely small rounding differences that may exist even in
single-line invoices, or as a minimum tolerance for all invoices.
Logic: If |System Calculated GST - Invoice GST| <= 0.05 , then
allow posting.
Purpose: To handle the most common, extremely small rounding differences
and avoid excessive blocking.
Tier 2: Dynamic Tolerance Based on Number of Line Items (for multi-line
invoices)
For invoices that fail Tier 1 validation, further check their number of line
items.
Logic: If Number of Invoice Line Items > 1 AND |System
Calculated GST - Invoice GST| <= (Number of Invoice Line
Items * Max_Line_Error_Threshold) , then allow posting.
Max_Line_Error_Threshold : This value needs to be calibrated based on
actual conditions. Based on your description, you can try setting it to 0.01.
This means we allow a maximum accumulated error of 0.01 per line.

Purpose: Specifically addresses reasonable larger discrepancies in multi-line
invoices due to accumulated rounding.
3.3. Comprehensive Validation Process
When Concur transmits invoice data to SAP, the system should perform the following
validation logic:
Calculate Expected GST: Based on the invoice's non-taxable amount and the 15%
GST rate, calculate the system's expected total GST amount (to multiple decimal
places).
Get Invoice GST: Obtain the total GST amount provided by the supplier from the
invoice data transmitted by Concur.
Calculate Difference: Difference = |System Expected GST - Supplier
Invoice GST| .
Validate Tier 1 Tolerance:
If Difference <= 0.05 , then Allow Posting.
Validate Tier 2 Tolerance:
If Difference > 0.05 , then further check:
Get the number of line items N on the invoice.
Calculate the dynamic tolerance upper limit:
Dynamic_Tolerance_Limit = N * 0.01 .
If Difference <= Dynamic_Tolerance_Limit , then Allow Posting.
Block Posting: If both of the above validations fail, then Block Posting, mark the
invoice as an exception, and trigger a manual review process.
3.4. Examples
Example 1: 200-line invoice, Difference 2.00
Difference = 2.00
Tier 1 validation: 2.00 > 0.05 , failed.
Tier 2 validation: N = 200 ,
Dynamic_Tolerance_Limit = 200 * 0.01 = 2.00 .
2.00 <= 2.00 , passed. Allow Posting.
Example 2: 1-line invoice, Difference 2.00
Difference = 2.00
Tier 1 validation: 2.00 > 0.05 , failed.
Tier 2 validation: N = 1 , Dynamic_Tolerance_Limit = 1 * 0.01 =
0.01 .
2.00 > 0.01 , failed. Block Posting.
Example 3: 5-line invoice, Difference 0.03
Difference = 0.03
Tier 1 validation: 0.03 <= 0.05 , passed. Allow Posting.
3.5. Key Calibration Parameters
Max_Line_Error_Threshold (0.01): This value is critical. It represents the
maximum acceptable rounding error per line in GST calculation. You need to
analyze the actual situation of supplier invoices and historical data to confirm if
0.01 is a reasonable upper limit. If the supplier's rounding logic is stricter, this value
can be smaller.
Absolute Fixed Tolerance (0.05): This value can be adjusted based on your
tolerance for extremely small discrepancies. It captures cases where there are very
few line items but still minor rounding differences.
4. Implementation Recommendations
Data Analysis and Calibration: Before implementation, conduct a detailed
analysis of historical invoice data, especially invoices with GST discrepancies.
Statistically analyze the distribution of actual GST discrepancies for different
numbers of line items to precisely calibrate Max_Line_Error_Threshold and
the absolute fixed tolerance.
System Implementation: Implement the two-tier validation rules described above
in the Concur to SAP interface or in SAP's custom validation logic. Ensure that the
number of line items on the invoice can be accurately obtained.
Exception Workflow: Establish a clear exception handling workflow. For invoices
blocked from posting, automatically notify relevant personnel for manual review
and processing.
Continuous Monitoring and Optimization: After go-live, continuously monitor
GST discrepancy situations and the number and reasons for blocked invoices.
Regularly review and adjust tolerance parameters based on actual operational
feedback and business needs to achieve the optimal balance.
Supplier Communication: For suppliers who frequently have unreasonable
discrepancies, it may be necessary to communicate with them to understand the
calculation and rounding logic of their invoicing systems, and to guide them
towards adopting calculation methods that are more compliant or transparent.

entry.title: Re: Partial Usage Decision in Quality Management SAP Grow
entry.content: Hi

entry.title: Re: How to do background scheduling with clean core, including Fiori apps, replacing se38 reports
entry.content:
Hi,

Is it possible to capture the job log as internal table similar to ALV display ? Currently only application log seems is possible but it is not intuitive when we want to display tabular format.

Best wishes,

Ramjee Korada

entry.title: Re: Partial Usage Decision in Quality Management SAP Grow
entry.content: Does it possible on other SAP Platforms then.? (Non SAP Grow....... ECC, S/4 etc)
